sections: Canonical artifacts, renderer-derived views, and Projections
HCM-0.6 exact shipped kind and root-profile data
Intake record and artifact candidate contracts
PR-009 — Charter questionnaire-shaped coverage and posture validation
First-party intake, renderers, and Projection posture
complete selected dependency and transition record

repo_truth: Fresh read-only review of the exact seven-entry HCM-2.2 planning subject. No HCM-2.2 implementation packet existed at entry. The proposed packet adds planning authority only and must be independently checked against the current control pack, released definition bytes, live Charter/Environment Inventory/flow/CLI carriers, HCM-2.1 precedent, and implementability. The released Project Authority 1.0 schema is minimal while the live rich Charter input remains Markdown-first. Inherit no clean conclusion.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay the sorted seven-entry manifest and aggregate fingerprint before review.
Review every byte of 00, 04, 06, HCM-2.2 SPEC, plan, unchecked todo, and planning proof wall.

Read unchanged 01, 02, 03, 05, 07, 08, the HCM-0.6 decision, selected HCM-2.1 record/packet/proof, exact definition assets, and live engine/compiler/flow/CLI/skill/template/test sources only as needed.

Run bounded read-only Markdown, link, Git, handoff/schema, source/API feasibility, exact-definition/version, lineage/atomicity, cross-platform, determinism, security, bridge, and classification checks.
Return findings first with exact file/line evidence through the built-in channel.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user task hops.
No Rust, Cargo, definition, template, fixture, test, runtime/product, generated-skill, pipeline, HCM-2.3+, Phase-3, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, contract, dock, or publication implementation.
Do not invent an alternate schema, intake, CLI, lineage store, transaction, lifecycle, renderer, profile, migration, compatibility mode, trigger, bridge, or sibling-family scope.
Do not treat baseline tests, packet detail, contingent status wording, or HCM-2.1 precedent as proof that the seven-entry subject is clean.
tasks: Invoke required skills in order, remain read-only, replay the exact seven-entry manifest/aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any byte differs.
Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order with exact file/line, violated authority, failure, smallest remediation, and missing or invalidated proof.

Verify the additive 1.1 schema/kind/profile and new intake/renderer/lifecycle/approval/waiver/trigger closure is compatible with uniform exact-definition identity, current definition loader/package architecture, HCM-0.6 decisions, and live selected-profile consumers.

Verify the canonical Charter shape and fifteen coverage mappings are internally total and consistent with capability bindings, source/authority rules, immutable intake/candidate/approval/promotion contracts, explicit unknowns, waivers, and targeted reassessment.

Verify CLI/skill acquisition and approval/promotion choreography has one stable candidate schema, no nested model/wizard/self-approval, a realistic atomic storage boundary, stable typed adapters, and no legacy dual truth.
Verify renderer/lifecycle, setup/doctor, Environment Inventory, flow/C03/C04, bridge, Windows, security, package/install, regression, and deletion proof are executable and sufficiently bounded.
Verify planning-only status, exact scope, unchecked todo, gate/classification ceiling, critical GitNexus warning, primary commit, and separate parent handoff/ledger closeout.
Return CLEAN only if no valid Critical or Required finding remains; otherwise CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest/aggregate, required authority, live definitions/source, or mandatory read-only verification cannot be reproduced.

Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than selecting unspecified semantic bytes, authority behavior, transaction claims, unsafe platform fallback, compatibility, pipeline, sibling-family, or later-slice behavior.
Return to the parent if valid remediation would require implementation now, released-definition mutation, broader product/default-set authority, HCM-2.3+, or global handoff/ledger mutation.
